name: Atlassian MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Atlassian MCP server providing unified access to Jira, Confluence, and Bitbucket — enabling AI agents to search, read, and interact with Atlassian's suite of developer and project management tools.
use_cases: Searching and reading Jira issues and project backlogs
Querying Confluence documentation and wiki pages for context
Reading Bitbucket repository metadata and pull requests
Cross-product search across Atlassian's tool suite from agents
Automating Jira issue creation from agent-detected problems
not_for: Atlassian Data Center / Server on-premises (cloud only for MCP)
Complex Atlassian administration requiring admin API
best_when: An agent needs unified access to Jira project data, Confluence docs, or Bitbucket code — especially in organizations using the full Atlassian suite.
avoid_when: You only use one Atlassian product — use the product-specific community MCP for simpler setup.

auth.notes: Atlassian OAuth 2.0 (3LO) for cloud products. Fine-grained scopes for each product (Jira, Confluence, Bitbucket) and operation. User-level API tokens also supported. OAuth is preferred for production agents.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 10 users across Jira, Confluence
pricing.notes: MCP server is open source. Atlassian subscription required. API access included in all plans.

performance.rate_limits: Atlassian rate limits apply per product; typically 50-200 req/min

known_agent_gotchas: OAuth 3LO requires user authorization flow — for agent use, prefer service account with API token
Product-specific rate limits are separate — Jira and Confluence have independent quotas
Cloud-only — Atlassian Data Center requires separate API configuration
Jira custom fields have instance-specific IDs — must be discovered per org
Confluence space keys are org-specific — agents need discovery step before targeting specific content
Search results across products use different query languages (JQL for Jira, CQL for Confluence)
agent_readiness.security_notes: Official Atlassian MCP with fine-grained OAuth scopes per product. Atlassian's security posture is enterprise-grade with SOC 2 Type II. HTTPS enforced across all products.

security_score_components.security_notes: Official Atlassian MCP with fine-grained OAuth 2.0 scopes. SOC 2 Type II, ISO 27001 certified. HTTPS enforced. Audit logging available.
